title: Platform: Implement triage-authority capability for TODD (scoped PATCH on non-responsive issues)
description:
## Platform Implementation: TODD Triage-Authority Capability

**Parent decision:** [FUL-4186](/FUL/issues/FUL-4186) — CEO approved Option 1: grant TODD a scoped triage-authority capability.

## What to build

Implement a platform-level capability that allows TODD (agent `cafa7831-645b-4a66-81be-6e6a811add67`) to PATCH issues it does not own, with strict constraints:

### Allowed PATCH fields (triage-authority scope)
- `status`
- `assigneeAgentId`
- `blockedByIssueIds`

### Prohibited via triage-authority
- `title`, `description`, `body`, documents, comments, attachments — no content changes

### Enforcement rules
1. **Non-responsive threshold:** The target issue's assigned agent must have had no activity (comment, status change, checkout, run record) for ≥15 minutes (1 heartbeat cycle minimum per Automated Task Cadence Policy)
2. **Self-assign prohibition:** TODD cannot assign issues to itself via this capability
3. **Terminal status protection:** No patches to issues with status `done` or `cancelled`
4. **Audit log required:** Every triage-authority PATCH must produce an audit record containing: issueId, old/new field values, TODD's agentId, TODD's runId, timestamp, and the stalen

description:

Phase 1 — Disposition SLA:

- Flag and correct:
  - no-path in_progress

Phase 2 — Routing recovery:

- Preserve non-terminal blockers; clear blockers only when all blockers are terminal.
- For all-done-blocked, move to todo + owner assignment + clear blockedByIssueIds.
- For real active blockers, leave blocked as-is.

Auth/conflict handling:

- Do not spin/retry mutation loops.
- Escalate t

title: Deploy middleware hardening to production: INTERNAL_TOKEN env, log permissions (FUL-4740)
description:
## Context

Code changes from [FUL-4740](/FUL/issues/FUL-4740) are complete in the working copy at `/opt/help2day/ollama-safety-middleware/` (or sync from company working copy). This issue covers the production host tasks.

## Tasks

### 1. Set INTERNAL_TOKEN in EnvironmentFile

Generate a strong token and add it to `/etc/help2day/safety-middleware.env`:

```bash
# Generate token
openssl rand -hex 32
# Add to EnvironmentFile (as root):
echo 'INTERNAL_TOKEN=<generated-value>' >> /etc/help2day/safety-middleware.env
chmod 640 /etc/help2day/safety-middleware.env
```

Do NOT echo the token value to logs or issue comments — store it only in the EnvironmentFile and propagate by name to Vercel.

### 2. Fix log file permissions (Security Lead advisory)

```bash
chown root:help2day-safety /var/log/help2day/ollama_middleware.log
chmod 640 /var/log/help2day/ollama_middleware.log
```

### 3. Deploy updated middleware files

Sync the updated `app.py` and `middleware.py` to `/opt/help2day/ollama-safety-middleware/` and restart the service:

```bash
systemctl restart help2day-safety-middleware
systemctl status help2day-safety-middleware
```

### 4. Smoke test

Verify unauthenticated requests retur

title: Configure Tailscale connectivity for safety middleware → Vercel access (FUL-4677 smoke test gate)
description:
## Context

[FUL-4677](/FUL/issues/FUL-4677) (Deploy help2day-safety-middleware) is complete except for the smoke test. The smoke test requires Vercel to call the safety middleware at http://100.64.8.47:8080. The service correctly binds to 127.0.0.1:8080 only (AC1), which means it is unreachable from Vercel serverless functions.

Board approval for connectivity approach is pending (see approval on FUL-4677).

## Decision needed from Grant (board approval)

| Option | Description | Requires Grant | Security notes |
|--------|-------------|----------------|----------------|
| A — Tailscale Serve + Vercel integration | Enable Tailscale Serve on tailnet; configure Vercel Tailscale integration; service accessible only within Tailscale network | Enable Tailscale Serve (admin console) + approve Vercel Tailscale integration setup | Preferred — private network only |
| B — Tailscale Funnel | Enable Tailscale Funnel on tailnet; service accessible via public HTTPS URL  | Enable Tailscale Funnel (admin console) | Simpler but publicly reachable — needs auth review |

## Your job (DevOps Lead, once Grant approves)

**If Option A approved:**
1. Grant enables Tailscale Serve on tailnet at https://

title: Configure Tailscale connectivity for safety middleware -> Vercel access (FUL-4677 smoke test gate)
description:
## Context

[FUL-4677](/FUL/issues/FUL-4677) is complete except the smoke test. The safety middleware binds to 127.0.0.1:8080 only (correct per AC1), but Vercel serverless functions cannot reach this address. Board approval for connectivity approach is pending on FUL-4677.

## Options for Grant

**Option A (Tailscale Serve + Vercel integration)**: Enable Tailscale Serve on tailnet, run tailscale serve on paperclipai, configure Vercel Tailscale integration. Service remains private. Preferred.

**Option B (Tailscale Funnel)**: Enable Tailscale Funnel, service exposed via public Tailscale HTTPS URL. Simpler but publicly reachable.

## Your job (DevOps Lead, once Grant approves)

Execute approved option, update HELP2DAY_CHAT_BACKEND_URL in Vercel, run smoke test (POST /api/chat), post evidence on [FUL-4668](/FUL/issues/FUL-4668).

title: Merge PR #66 (Node.js 24 actions upgrade) before June 16 deadline
description:
## Summary

GitHub is forcing migration from Node.js 20 to Node.js 24 runtime for Actions jobs on **June 16, 2026** (18 days away). PR #66 in `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day` upgrades all 7 affected workflows.

**PR:** https://github.com/gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day/pull/66
**Branch:** `fix/ful-4724-node24-actions-upgrade` → `staging`
**FUL:** [FUL-4724](/FUL/issues/FUL-4724)

## What changed in PR #66

Upgrades `actions/checkout@v4→v5` and `actions/setup-node@v4→v5` across:
- `ci-cd.yml` (8 pins)
- `configure-chat-backend.yml`
- `daily-ai-spend-alert.yml`
- `deploy-help2day-backend.yml`
- `gitleaks.yml`
- `test-gate12-discord.yml`
- `verify-vercel-project.yml`

## Required action

1. Verify CI passes on the PR branch (test plan items need checking).
2. Review and approve PR #66.
3. Merge to `staging` before June 16.

## Stop condition

PR #66 merged to staging with CI green.

title: Upgrade GitHub Actions to Node.js 24 runtime before June 16 forced migration (help2day-web)
description:
## Summary

GitHub Actions is **forcing all workflows to Node.js 24 on June 16, 2026** (18 days from now). `actions/checkout@v4` and `actions/setup-node@v4` both use the Node.js 20 runtime and will be deprecated on that date.

**Deadline:** June 16, 2026 — hard deadline. Node.js 20 removed from runners September 16, 2026.

**Affected repo:** `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day`
**Affected workflows:** `ci-cd`, `verify-vercel-project`, `Secret Scan (gitleaks)` — all three show the warning.

## Required actions

1. Update `actions/checkout@v4` → `v5` (or confirm v4 supports Node.js 24)
2. Update `actions/setup-node@v4` → `v5`
3. Verify all workflows pass after update

Alternative short-term: Add `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` to test compatibility first.

## Evidence

From run `26660145649` on `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day` (staging branch, 2026-05-29T20:20Z UTC):
```
[warning] Node.js 20 actions are deprecated. The following actions are running on Node.js 20 and may not work as expected: actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4. Actions will be forced to run with Node.js 24 by default starting June 16th, 2026.
```
Same warning appears in `notify_preview_deploy_authority` and

title: Review and merge PR #66: Node.js 24 GH Actions upgrade (FUL-4724)
description:
## Task

Review and merge [PR #66](https://github.com/gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day/pull/66) into the `staging` branch.

**Deadline:** June 16, 2026 (GitHub forced migration date)

### What this PR does
Upgrades all `actions/checkout@v4` and `actions/setup-node@v4` pins to v5 across 7 workflows, eliminating Node.js 20 deprecation warnings.

### Acceptance criteria
1. CI passes on PR #66 with no Node.js 20 deprecation warnings in any job
2. PR merged to `staging` before June 16, 2026
3. Post merge SHA here and mark done

### Commit
- Branch: `fix/ful-4724-node24-actions-upgrade`
- Commit: `ecce311`
- Workflows: ci-cd, configure-chat-backend, daily-ai-spend-alert, deploy-help2day-backend, gitleaks, test-gate12-discord, verify-vercel-project

### Tier: T2

title: [FUL-4792] Security: Investigate gitleaks finding on help2day fix/ful-4728 branch
description:
## Context

During the post-push CI review ([FUL-4792](/FUL/issues/FUL-4792)), a gitleaks scan detected a hardcoded secret on branch `fix/ful-4728-node24-actions-clean` in `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day`.

**Run:** 26662595448 (2026-05-29T21:15)
**Finding:** `leaks found: 1`

## What we know

- PR #67 (`FUL-4728: clean Node.js 24 Actions upgrade PR`) was **CLOSED without merging** — the secret did NOT enter `main` or `staging`.
- A subsequent commit on the same branch at 23:09 passed gitleaks, indicating the value was removed from HEAD.
- The original commit containing the leaked value still exists in the branch's git history on GitHub.

## Required actions

1. **Identify** the secret type from the gitleaks artifact in run 26662595448 (check the full gitleaks report output — the warning only shows "leaks found: 1" at the CLI level; the SARIF/JSON report artifact will have the file, line, and rule).
2. **Verify rotation** — if the leaked value is a live credential (API key, token, webhook secret), rotate it immediately.
3. **Assess git-history cleanup** — if the value is sensitive, evaluate whether BFG Repo Cleaner or `git filter-repo` is needed to purge it from GitHub's object store

title: Create clean PR for Node.js 24 GH Actions upgrade only (FUL-4724)
description:
Parent: [FUL-4726](/FUL/issues/FUL-4726)

Problem:
PR #66 is not merge-safe for a CI-only upgrade. It is `DIRTY`, diverged from `staging` (44 behind / 19 ahead), and includes unrelated backend/frontend/security changes.

Required deliverable:
1. Create a fresh branch from current `staging`.
2. Apply ONLY FUL-4724 scope: bump `actions/checkout@v4 -> v5` and `actions/setup-node@v4 -> v5` across workflow files.
3. Do not include app/backend runtime code, AGENTS.md policy edits, gitleaks suppression, scripts, or assets.
4. Open replacement PR and link it in parent issue.

Run every 30 minutes.

Primary rule:
- Use direct PATCH for routine disposition and routing corrections.
- Do NOT create CEO triage patch-batch issues for normal stale/disposition routing.
- Escalation issues are only for policy exceptions, irreversible actions, explicit board decisions, or CTO-level architecture/cross-domain intervention.

Phase 1 — Disposition SLA:
- Scan in_progress, in_review, blocked.
- Flag and correct:
  - no-path in_progress
  - reviewer-less in_review (after interaction guard)
  - ownerless/orphaned blocked
- Apply board-waiting hard guard before any mutation/comment.
- Respect pending interaction waiting postures.

Phase 2 — Routing recovery:
- Detect routing mismatch and assign to accountable owner directly.
- Preserve non-terminal blockers; clear blockers only when all blockers are terminal.
- For all-done-blocked, move to todo + owner assignment + clear blockedByIssueIds.
- For real active blockers, leave blocked as-is.

Auth/conflict handling:
- First 403/401 or 409 on an issue ends mutation attempts for that issue in this run.
- Do not spin/retry mutation loops.
- Escalate t

title: Fix missing safety function imports on staging branch (help2day ci-cd typecheck failure)
description:
## Problem

The `staging` branch in `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day` has had repeated `ci-cd` typecheck failures (2× on 2026-05-29: 19:39 UTC and 23:18 UTC).

**6 TS2304 errors in `app/api/chat/route.ts`** — safety functions referenced but not imported:
- Line 343: `detectChildDataIndicators`
- Line 356: `CHILD_DATA_ESCALATION_MESSAGE`
- Line 522: `detectStalkingSpyware`
- Line 531: `SAFETY_RESOURCES_RESPONSE`
- Line 561: `buildIncidentResponseTemplate`
- Line 566: `incidentTicketPriority`

These are safety/child-protection filter functions. Their absence from staging means safety filters may not be deployed there.

## Required action

1. Identify where these functions are defined in `main` (likely a safety utilities module).
2. Add the missing import(s) to `staging`'s `app/api/chat/route.ts`.
3. Verify `npm run typecheck` passes on staging.
4. Push fix to staging; confirm CI green.

## Stop condition

`staging` branch `ci-cd` typecheck step passes green.

## Tier: T2

**Validation:** CI run link showing green typecheck on staging.

Escalated from CI review [FUL-4784](/FUL/issues/FUL-4784).

title: Fix Vercel deployment authorization for PR #64 — @Paperclip-Paperclip must join gboyles2paperclipai Vercel team
description:
## Problem

Vercel bot is blocking preview deployments for PR #64 on `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day` because `@Paperclip-Paperclip` is not a member of the `gboyles2paperclipai's projects` team on Vercel.

Both projects are blocked:

* `help2day-web`

Additional finding: Vercel CLI on `paperclip01` is logged in as `gboyles2paperclipai`, active team `gboyles2paperclipais-projects`, but `vercel project ls` returns "No projects found" — this conflicts with known existing deployments. Possible causes: wrong team scope, stale CLI auth, CLI permission mismatch, project transfer issue, or integration-only visibility.

## Immediate Objectives

1. Identify the exact PR #64 deploy-author identity Vercel is evaluating (author, committer, co-author, GitHub App actor, or deployment initiator).
2. Confirm what role `@Paperclip-Paperclip` plays in the PR (author/committer/co-author/GitHub App actor).
3. Determine the **least-privilege unblock** — prefer in this order:

## Problem

The `verify_preview_deploy_status` job in help2day CI is reporting Vercel deployments as BLOCKED before build starts. This failure appeared on **2 different branches** within 27 minutes, meeting the repeated-failure escalation threshold.

### Failing runs
- Run [26658312532](https://github.com/gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day/actions/runs/26658312532) — `staging` branch, SHA `cef417f`, 19:39 UTC
- Run [26656974813](https://github.com/gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day/actions/runs/26656974813) — `fix/ful-4686-remove-pnpm-action-setup` PR, SHA `299ab70`, 19:12 UTC

### Error from log
```
Deployment BLOCKED at https://help2day-aa522ue4i-gboyles2paperclipais-projects.vercel.app
ACTION REQUIRED: Vercel blocked this deployment before building started.
Known causes: a prior '\''vercel deploy --prod'\'' CLI call (no git SHA) overriding the Git integration production pointer;
  a project-level Git integration conflict; or account-level restrictions (billing, TOS).
To resolve: go to https://vercel.com/dashboard → help2day-web → Deployments and check the blocked deployment details.
```

### Notes
- The `staging` branch subsequently succeeded on its next push at 20:19 UTC (run 26660145649)

title: DEADLINE: Resolve merge conflicts on PR #66 for Node.js 24 upgrade (June 16, 2026)
description:
## Context

PR #66 (`fix/ful-4724-node24-actions-upgrade`, [FUL-4724](/FUL/issues/FUL-4724)) is currently showing `mergeable=CONFLICTING`. This PR upgrades all GH Actions to Node.js 24 runtime (actions/checkout v4→v5, actions/setup-node v4→v5) ahead of the **June 16, 2026** GitHub forced migration deadline.

PR #66 merged to main with green CI.

title: Vercel chat route: add X-Internal-Token header to middleware requests (FUL-4740)
description:
## Context

[FUL-4740](/FUL/issues/FUL-4740) adds an `X-Internal-Token` auth gate to the help2day-safety-middleware FastAPI app. The Vercel TypeScript chat route must pass the token header on every request to the middleware, otherwise all requests return HTTP 401.

## Required change

In the TypeScript chat route that sets `HELP2DAY_CHAT_BACKEND_URL` (the proxy path to the middleware), add the `X-Internal-Token` header on every fetch call to the middleware:

```typescript
const middlewareToken = process.env.MIDDLEWARE_INTERNAL_TOKEN ?? '';
// When proxying to middleware:
headers: {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'X-Internal-Token': middlewareToken,
}
```

The env var `MIDDLEWARE_INTERNAL_TOKEN` will be set in Vercel by DevOps Lead after they generate the token on the server ([FUL-4743](/FUL/issues/FUL-4743)).

## Acceptance criteria

- All requests from the Vercel route to `https://paperclipai.tailbf5b41.ts.net/api/chat` include `X-Internal-Token: <token>`
- Requests succeed end-to-end (200 response from middleware)
- No token value is logged or exposed in Vercel logs

## Dependency

`MIDDLEWARE_INTERNAL_TOKEN` Vercel env var must be set by DevOps Lead ([FUL-4743](/FUL/iss

title: [SECURITY] Admin session cookie: replace raw password value with opaque session token
description:
## Production Gate: Admin session cookie must use session token (not raw password)

**Parent:** [FUL-4588](/FUL/issues/FUL-4588) — CEO/CTO Preview Readiness Review  
**Gate:** PROD-7 (must fix before public launch; strongly recommended before preview)

### Problem
`app/api/admin/login/route.ts` sets the session cookie value to the raw `ADMIN_PASSWORD` value (line ~47). Middleware then reads the cookie value and compares it against the password.

This means:
- If any logging, debugging, or monitoring ever captures cookie values, the password is exposed
- The pattern is fragile — any cookie inspection reveals the credential
- Industry standard: session cookies should contain opaque random tokens validated against a server-side store

### Fix
1. On login, generate a cryptographically random session token (`crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')` or similar)
2. Store the token in a server-side Map (or Vercel KV for persistence)
3. Set the cookie to the token value (not the password)
4. Middleware reads the cookie token, looks up the token in the store, grants access if found and not expired
5. On logout, delete the token from the store
6. Keep the 30-minute maxAge expiry behavior

###

title: Fix: replace in-memory admin session store with HMAC token (serverless-compatible)
description:
## Bug

The admin session store in `lib/admin-session-store.ts` uses an in-memory `Map<string, AdminSessionRecord>`. This is incompatible with Vercel's serverless/edge architecture:

- `POST /api/admin/login` (Node.js runtime) — writes session token to the in-memory Map
- `middleware.ts` (Edge Runtime) — reads from its **own isolated, empty** Map

Result: every `/admin/` request sees an empty session store and returns 401, even immediately after a successful login. Confirmed on staging at 2026-05-29T21:10Z via [FUL-4719](/FUL/issues/FUL-4719) smoke test.

## Required Fix (MVP scope)

**HMAC signed token (recommended — zero infrastructure):**

1. Add `ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET` env var (random 32-byte hex) to Vercel staging + production.
2. `POST /api/admin/login`: generate a signed cookie `HMAC-SHA256(token+expiry, ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET)` — compact string, self-contained.
3. `middleware.ts`: verify HMAC + expiry inline; no server-side store lookup.
4. Remove `lib/admin-session-store.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `GET /admin/` with valid session cookie → 200 or non-auth redirect (currently 401)
- Middleware does not require Node.js-only modules
- `ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET` provisioned by In

title: FUL-4700: Reconfigure help2day git push identity on paperclip01 from Paperclip-Paperclip to gboyles2paperclipAI
description:
## Context

[FUL-4700](/FUL/issues/FUL-4700) — Vercel deployments are `BLOCKED` because the git pusher identity on `paperclip01` for the `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day` repo is `Paperclip-Paperclip` (GitHub ID 271391591). Vercel evaluates the **pusher identity** against the Vercel team authorizations, and `Paperclip-Paperclip` is not a member of the `gboyles2paperclipai` Vercel team.

Grant's Vercel account (`gboyles2paperclipai`) is already connected to GitHub as `gboyles2paperclipAI` — his own account — which IS authorized. The fix is to reconfigure the help2day push identity on `paperclip01` to use `gboyles2paperclipAI`'s credentials.

Grant explicitly authorized this fix:
> "Change the agent commit/deploy author identity so Vercel evaluates gboyles2paperclipAI, not Paperclip-Paperclip."

## Deliverable

Reconfigure the `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day` git push credentials on `paperclip01` so all agent pushes authenticate as `gboyles2paperclipAI` (not `Paperclip-Paperclip`).

## Stop Condition

Pushing a test commit to a branch of `gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day` from `paperclip01` results in a Vercel deployment with status `READY` (not `BLOCKED`), with the pusher evaluated as `gboyl

title: Provision ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET in Vercel (staging + production) for HMAC admin sessions
description:
## Task

Generate and provision `ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET` in Vercel `help2day-web` project for both **staging** (preview) and **production** environments.

This is required by [FUL-4729](/FUL/issues/FUL-4729) — HMAC admin session store fix.

## Steps

1. Generate a secure 32-byte random hex value: `node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(32).toString('hex'))"` (run locally — do NOT log the value)
2. Add `ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET` to Vercel `help2day-web` project:
   - Target: `preview` AND `production`
   - Type: `sensitive`
3. Verify by name only: `curl https://api.vercel.com/v9/projects/help2day-web/env` — check key present, never print value
4. Comment back: `ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET: present, target=[preview,production], length=64`

## Safety constraints

- Do NOT print the secret value
- Do NOT log it to any comment or stdout
- Validate by name + length only

## Tier: T2 (Infrastructure Lead)

No Grant approval needed — this is an internal admin session secret, not a new external service.

description:
## Staging admin-login smoke test — FUL-4678

### Context

The opaque session token fix is live on `staging` (commit `a625aa3`). `ADMIN_PASSWORD` has been provisioned by Grant in Vercel help2day-web Preview (staging scope) and Production. The redeployment is complete per Grant's confirmation at 2026-05-29T20:56Z.

title: Unblock Vercel preview deployments — Git integration BLOCKED (repeated CI failure)
description:

3. If caused by a p

title: Infrastructure Lead: deployment plan for help2day-safety-middleware — command allowlist + rollback plan
description:
## Context

This is a prerequisite planning issue for [FUL-4677](/FUL/issues/FUL-4677) (Deploy help2day-safety-middleware on paperclipai host).

The board has rejected two consecutive request_confirmation interactions on [FUL-4677](/FUL/issues/FUL-4677) that asked for "temporary passwordless sudo for an agent runtime" without a concrete plan. Grant's direction: **present a tight command allowlist and rollback/validation plan before any sudoers rule discussion**.

## Deliverable

Produce a deployment plan document on this issue that includes ALL of the following:

### 1. Exact command allowlist
List the precise sudo commands the paperclipadmin (or help2day-safety) service account needs on paperclipai to execute the middleware deployment. No wildcards. Example format:

```
paperclipadmin ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/mkdir -p /opt/help2day/*
paperclipadmin ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/sbin/useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin help2day-safety
paperclipadmin ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl daemon-reload
paperclipadmin ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl enable help2day-safety-middleware
paperclipadmin ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl start help2day-sa

title: Fix PR Quality Gate lint failure — add ESLint config to help2day (blocks PR #63)
description:
## Fix-Forward: PR Quality Gate FAILED due to missing ESLint config

**Observed in:** help2day GitHub PR #63 (`feat/ful-4659-pr-quality-gate`), CI run 26654830982 (2026-05-29)
**Failure pattern:** `npm run lint --if-present` → `next lint` → ESLint config not found → Next.js launches interactive setup wizard → fails in CI (non-interactive) with exit code 1
**Root cause:** The help2day project has no `.eslintrc.*` or `eslint.config.*` file. When `next lint` runs without a config it prompts interactively, which fails in CI.
**Proposed fix (two options):**
1. Add a minimal `eslint.config.mjs` (or `.eslintrc.json`) extending `next/core-web-vitals` to the help2day repo root (preferred — adds real lint coverage)
2. Modify the PR Quality Gate workflow to skip lint if no ESLint config is present (`[ -f .eslintrc* ] || [ -f eslint.config* ] && npm run lint --if-present`)

**Recurrence risk if not fixed:** High — PR #63 cannot be merged until CI passes. Any future PR on this repo will also fail the lint step.
**Owner:** Frontend Lead (add ESLint config) or DevOps Lead (guard the workflow step)

**Evidence:**
- Run: https://github.com/gboyles2paperclipAI/help2day/actions/runs/26654830982
- PR:

title: [PREVIEW GATE] Restrict health endpoints to prevent provider/model info disclosure
description:
## Preview Gate Blocker PG-3: Health Endpoint Information Disclosure

**Parent:** [FUL-4588](/FUL/issues/FUL-4588) — CEO/CTO Preview Readiness Review

### Problem
`/api/health` and `/api/health/chat` expose LLM provider name, model version, and paid-fallback enabled/disabled status to any unauthenticated caller. This reveals infrastructure details to potential attackers.

Current unauthenticated response includes:
- Active LLM provider (e.g., "local_ollama", "anthropic")
- Model identifier (e.g., "msp-helpdesk:latest")
- Whether paid fallback is enabled
- Readiness state

### Fix
Restrict detailed health data to callers that present the `INTERNAL_NOTIFICATIONS_SECRET` header (same pattern used by internal notification routes). Unauthenticated callers should receive only a minimal `{ "status": "ok" }` response.

### Acceptance criteria
- `GET /api/health` without auth header returns `{ "status": "ok" }` only
- `GET /api/health/chat` without auth header returns `{ "status": "ok" }` only
- `GET /api/health/chat` with valid `x-internal-secret: <INTERNAL_NOTIFICATIONS_SECRET>` returns full detail
- CI uptime check still passes (update check to include auth header if needed)
- TypeScript

title: [PREVIEW GATE] Add robots.ts (Disallow: /) and fix metadataBase placeholder domain
description:
## Preview Gate Blockers PG-1 and PG-2

**Parent:** [FUL-4588](/FUL/issues/FUL-4588) — CEO/CTO Preview Readiness Review

### PG-1: Create app/robots.ts
Create `app/robots.ts` returning `{ rules: { userAgent: '*', disallow: '/' } }` until production-ready. Without this, Google can index the site immediately if Vercel preview protection is ever lifted.

### PG-2: Fix metadataBase placeholder
Replace the hardcoded `https://help2day.example` placeholder in `app/layout.tsx` with an env var: `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL`. Add `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` to `.env.example` and set in Vercel environment.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `app/robots.ts` exists and returns Disallow: / for all user agents
- `app/layout.tsx` metadataBase uses env var (not hardcoded placeholder)
- `.env.example` includes NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL
- TypeScript builds without errors
- PR passes CI

title: Greptile P1: Unbounded in-memory cache with no eviction — PR #6985
description:
Source PR: https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/6985

Detected unresolved Greptile P1 review comment by `greptile-apps[bot]`.

Finding details:
- Path: `server/src/routes/agents.ts`
- Line: 3132
- Greptile comment id: `3324459772`
- Summary: `liveRunsCache` is a module-level map with TTL-only-on-read and no global eviction/sweep, creating unbounded growth risk over long-lived process lifetimes.

Bot fix prompt excerpt:
- Consider adding capped map size (LRU eviction) or periodic sweep to remove expired entries.

Routing rationale:
- Backend/server behavior issue; default route is Backend Lead.

Policy mapping:
- Priority: `high` for P1.
- Dedup key: PR #6985 + Greptile comment id 3324459772.

Auth/conflict handling:
- First 403/401 or 409 on an issue ends mutation attempts for that issue in this run.
- Do not spin/retry mutation loops.
- Escalate t

title: Deploy Help2day local-ollama private preview (FUL-4552 grant-approved)
description:
## Context

Grant approved the FUL-4552 MVP path ([FUL-4552](/FUL/issues/FUL-4552)). FUL-4582 code (`b914a4a` on `feat/ful-3454-aws-oidc`) is ready. This issue covers the 3-step private preview deployment.

**Scope: private preview only. NOT production. NOT staging. NOT public.**

## Steps

### Step 1 — Set Vercel private-preview env vars

Set in the Vercel project for the private preview deployment (NOT production or staging):
```
HELP2DAY_CHAT_ENABLED=false
HELP2DAY_CHAT_PROVIDER=local_ollama
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://paperclipai:11434
OLLAMA_CHAT_MODEL=msp-helpdesk:latest
PAID_MODEL_FALLBACK_ENABLED=false
```

`HELP2DAY_CHAT_ENABLED=false` is the kill switch default. Grant sets it to `true` when ready to test chat.

### Step 2 — Merge feat/ful-3454-aws-oidc to a private preview branch

- Create branch `preview/local-ollama` from `feat/ful-3454-aws-oidc`
- Do NOT merge to `staging` or `main`

### Step 3 — Deploy to Vercel preview URL

- Trigger Vercel preview deploy on `preview/local-ollama` branch
- Confirm the preview URL is NOT the production domain
- Post the Vercel preview URL to this issue for Grant review

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Env vars set in Vercel private preview e

title: Fix Vercel compile blocker for private-preview/local-ollama deploy (FUL-4604 unblock)
description:
## Blocker Context

FUL-4604 private-preview deploy is blocked by a build failure on approved commit `b914a4a`:

- `Module not found: Can't resolve '@/lib/observability'`
- Vercel result: `deploy_failed` / `Command "npm run build" exited with 1`

## Required Backend Action

1. Fix missing import/module path so the approved private-preview source builds successfully on Vercel.
2. Keep scope limited to compile unblock for `private-preview/local-ollama` deployment path.
3. Push fix to the private-preview branch path used by FUL-4604.
4. Post build-pass evidence (run/deploy URL + timestamp, redacted, no secrets).

## Unblock Condition

FUL-4604 is unblocked when preview deploy builds successfully and DevOps can execute protected-preview/noindex/runtime evidence checks.

title: Fix compile error: Can't resolve '@/lib/observability' on private-preview/local-ollama source
description:

Scope
- Work from source commit lineage approved in FUL-4604 (`feat/ful-3454-aws-oidc`, commit b914a4a lineage) and apply the minimum code change needed to resolve `Module not found: Can't resolve '@/lib/observability'` during Next.js build for private-preview/local-ollama path.
- Keep this strictly to compile unblock; no deploy and no env/secret changes.

Required steps
1. Reproduce build failure with `npm run build` in the target branch/worktree.
2. Implement minimal fix for missing module path/import resolution.
3. Re-run `npm run build` and confirm success.
4. Commit and push the fix branch used by FUL-4604 private-preview flow.

Stop condition
- `npm run build` passes on the corrected source branch and evidence is posted on this child issue.

Validation requirement
- Backend Lead verifies evidence quality and then routes downstream to DevOps for preview deployment evidence in FUL-4604.

title: P1-B: Delete stale Vercel .env.preview.local (world-readable) in ful257-gate12-clone workspace
description:
## Source
Security audit [FUL-4598](/FUL/issues/FUL-4598) — P1-B finding. SH-9 violation.

## Finding
- File: `workspaces/ful257-gate12-clone/.vercel/.env.preview.local`
- Permissions: `0644` (world-readable) — should be `600` or deleted
- Size: 2015 bytes — Vercel CLI generated, likely contains preview env vars
- Created: 2026-05-27 — stale issue workspace

## Tasks
1. Determine if workspace `ful257-gate12-clone` is still needed
2. If not: delete the entire workspace directory
3. If needed: at minimum `chmod 600` the `.vercel/.env.preview.local` file
4. Confirm whether `VERCEL_PROTECTION_BYPASS` is in this file and if so, rotate it
5. Add enforcement: after any `vercel pull`, auto-`chmod 600` the resulting `.env.*.local` files

## Rules
- Do NOT cat or display the file contents (SH-9)
- Rotation evidence required if bypass token is present: timestamp + confirming URL probe (no token value)

## Acceptance criteria
- File deleted or has `chmod 600` permissions
- VERCEL_PROTECTION_BYPASS rotated if it was present in this file
- Post-Vercel-pull chmod hardening documented

title: Fix 3 blocking conditions before ollama-safety-middleware private preview integration
description:
## Context

Security review [FUL-4583](/FUL/issues/FUL-4583) passed the ollama-safety-middleware with PASS WITH CONDITIONS. Three blocking conditions must be resolved before Help2day app integration can proceed.

## B1 — Log file permissions
ollama_middleware.log is 644 (world-readable). Required: chmod 600 + startup procedure; log dir 750.

## B2 — No log rotation
Replace logging.FileHandler with RotatingFileHandler (10 MB / 5 backups). 30-day retention policy.

## B3 — gate_context must never be user-supplied (HIGHEST RISK)
Approval gate reads request.metadata.get(gate_context) at middleware.py:374. If the integration passes raw user metadata, attacker can forge gate_context to flip DENY to ALLOW_WITH_CONDITIONS on any risky action. Fix: strip gate_context at integration boundary; only backend populates it.

## Stop Condition
All three fixes implemented, tests pass, Security Lead confirms before integration merge.

## Non-Blocking (before GA)
- Remove StreamHandler in production (middleware.py:23)
- Hash user_id before logging (middleware.py:427)
- Read OLLAMA_BASE_URL from config.json
- Document OLLAMA_API_KEY no-auth is Tailscale-gated

title: BUG — ollama-safety-middleware log redaction exposes credential VALUES not just keywords
description:
## Bug Report

From [FUL-4552](/FUL/issues/FUL-4552) discovery. **Severity: HIGH. Must fix before private preview use.**

## Evidence

From `ollama_middleware.log`:
```
"prompt_redacted": "my [REDACTED] is SuperSecret123 and [REDACTED] 123456"
```

The original prompt was likely: `"my password is SuperSecret123 and mfa code 123456"`

The redaction replaced the keywords (`password`, `mfa code`) with `[REDACTED]` but left the actual VALUES (`SuperSecret123`, `123456`) in plaintext in the log.

## Impact

- Log file stores literal credential values
- Anyone with log file access can read submitted credentials
- The blocking and delivery suppression work correctly — but the log record itself is the exposure

## Fix Required

In `middleware.py`, the redaction function must:
1. Identify the credential keyword pattern match
2. Also redact the VALUE that follows the keyword (e.g. the word/token after `password is`, `token is`, `key is`)
3. Preserve the redacted structure for audit (e.g. `"my [REDACTED-KEYWORD] is [REDACTED-VALUE] and [REDACTED-KEYWORD] [REDACTED-VALUE]"`)

Alternatively, for maximum safety: redact any token within N words of a matched credential keyword.

## Acceptance Crit

description:

Run every 15 minutes before standard triage.

Primary rule:

title: Restore _CTO and _Security Lead from error state
description:
Both _CTO (1320f476) and _Security Lead (ea4d40ac) show status=error. These are critical leadership agents whose error state blocks architecture decisions, security reviews, and sprint execution.

## Required

1. Diagnose the error state for both agents — check adapter config, last run logs, and whether the claude_local adapter is failing
2. Restore both agents to idle/active status
3. Verify each can accept and process a simple test issue
4. Report findings as evidence before closing

## Context

CTO appeared to be running on FUL-4336 (executionLockedAt set) suggesting partial operation. Security Lead error may be a different cause.

## Acceptance criteria

- _CTO status = idle or active (not error)
- _Security Lead status = idle or active (not error)
- Evidence of successful heartbeat run for each posted in comments

Parent: [FUL-4551](/FUL/issues/FUL-4551)

## Stop condition (observable)
- _CTO (1320f476) status is `idle` or `active` (not `error`)
- _Security Lead (ea4d40ac) status is `idle` or `active` (not `error`)
- One successful post-recovery execution signal is recorded for each agent (heartbeat/test issue activity) with timestamps

title: Security Lead Review — ollama-safety-middleware before private preview
description:
## Context

The ollama-safety-middleware POC ([FUL-4552](/FUL/issues/FUL-4552)) is awaiting Security Lead review before any private preview integration with the Help2day app.

## Scope of Review

Location: `companies/24c167fc.../help2day/ollama-safety-middleware/`

Review and confirm:
- [ ] Classification patterns (BLOCKED/CHANGE/ESCALATE/READ_ONLY) are correct and safe
- [ ] Approval gate logic (G5, G8, G11, G12) is sound
- [ ] Stalking detection covers both victim and perpetrator perspectives correctly
- [ ] Fail-closed behavior verified: unevaluable checks deny by default
- [ ] No hardcoded cloud/online fallback
- [ ] Canned responses for BLOCKED, ESCALATE, and stalking are appropriate
- [ ] Injection/jailbreak patterns are sufficient
- [ ] Logging does not expose sensitive data beyond what is acceptable (see NOTE below)
- [ ] config.json hardcoded values (Ollama IP 100.64.8.47) are appropriate for private preview use

**NOTE — Known Bug (must assess):** The redaction in `ollama_middleware.log` replaces credential KEYWORDS with `[REDACTED]` but leaves the actual VALUES in plaintext (e.g. `"my [REDACTED] is SuperSecret123"`). A separate fix issue exists. Security Lead must confir

title: Disposition SLA + Routing Recovery
description:

Run every 30 minutes.

Primary rule:
- Use direct PATCH for routine disposition and routing corrections.
- Do NOT create CEO triage patch-batch issues for normal stale/disposition routing.

Phase 1 — Disposition SLA:

- Flag and correct:

- Apply board-waiting hard guard before any mutation/comment.

- Preserve non-terminal blockers; clear blockers only when all blockers are terminal.
- For all-done-blocked, move to todo + owner assignment + clear blockedByIssueIds.
- For real active blockers, leave blocked as-is.

Auth/conflict handling:
- First 403/401 or 409 on an issue ends mutation attempts for that issue in this run.
- Do not spin/retry mutation loops.
- Escalate t

title: Audit and deactivate orphaned routines from defunct TODD agent (cafa7831)
description:
Active routines exist owned by agent ID cafa7831 which no longer appears in the agent roster. This agent was likely the previous TODD instance that was recreated as b73b1543 (TODD-Ticket Router).

## Orphaned routines observed

- Stuck Ticket Scanner (*/15 cron)
- Disposition SLA + Routing Recovery (*/30 cron)
- FUL-3505-B confirmation watcher (api trigger)
- TODD Deep Stuck-Ticket Audit (0 9 */2 cron)

## Current TODD (b73b1543) has overlapping routines

- Proactive Run Health + Fix Validation Review (0 */4 *)
- TODD 2-Hour Ops-Pulse — Platform Health Check (0 */2 *)

## Required

1. Query the routines API to confirm which routines are owned by cafa7831
2. Determine if these routines overlap with b73b1543 routines
3. Either (a) migrate needed routines to b73b1543 or (b) deactivate them
4. Ensure no duplicate scanning loops remain active
5. Verify TODD (b73b1543) has complete coverage after cleanup

## Risk

Orphaned routines may still execute but have no owner agent to process results. Duplicate scanning wastes budget and creates noise.

title: TODD: Implement Daily Health Check + Weekly Digest Routines
description:
## Objective

Implement two recurring routines to replace manual CEO/CTO process oversight with automated exception-based reporting.

---

## Routine 1: Daily Agent & Issue Health Check

**Schedule:** Every 24 hours (`0 9 * * *` UTC)
**Model:** Local/cheap (haiku or local)
**Owner:** TODD-Ticket Router

### Check list (read-only, no mutations unless explicit)

1. **Stale in-progress issues** — `in_progress` with no update in >24h → list with owner
2. **Loop detection** — issues re-opened >2x in 7 days without new evidence comment → flag
3. **Unassigned `todo` issues** — no assignee >2h → flag with recommended owner
4. **Blocked >48h without named unblock owner** → flag to assignee
5. **Runaway agent comments** — >10 comments on same issue in 24h without status change → noise flag
6. **Agent last active** — any agent with no run in >72h that has `in_progress` issues → flag

### Output

- If zero exceptions: post nothing (silent-pass)
- If exceptions found: post Discord summary via `$DISCORD_EXEC_WEBHOOK_URL` with structured exception list
- Each exception must name: issue ID, owner, specific problem, recommended action

---

## Routine 2: Weekly Sprint Digest

**Schedule:** Every Mo

title: Daily Agent & Issue Health Check (Exceptions-Only)
description: Daily read-only scan for stale/looping/unassigned/blocked/noisy activity. Silent pass when zero exceptions; when exceptions exist, post structured Discord summary to DISCORD_EXEC_WEBHOOK_URL with recommended actions.

title: Upgrade GitHub Actions to Node.js 24 before June 2nd forced deprecation
description:
## Urgency: June 2nd, 2026 (4 days from now)

**Why:** GitHub Actions is forcing Node.js 24 as the default for action scripts on June 2nd, 2026. The following actions are deprecated running on Node.js 20 and may behave unexpectedly:
- `actions/checkout@v4`
- `actions/setup-node@v4`
- `pnpm/action-setup@v4`

This was observed in CI logs on PR paperclipai/paperclip#6934 on 2026-05-29.

**Timeline:**
- June 2nd, 2026: Node.js 24 becomes forced default for action scripts
- September 16th, 2026: Node.js 20 removed from runner entirely

**Impact:** All GitHub Actions workflows in `paperclipai/paperclip` and `gboyles2paperclipAI/paperclip` that use these action versions. CI could break or behave unexpectedly after June 2nd.

## Fix Options

**Option A (immediate, low-risk):** Add `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` environment variable to all workflow files to opt in now before the forced rollout.

**Option B (preferred, durable):** Upgrade to newer action versions that natively support Node.js 24.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No Node.js 20 deprecation warnings in CI logs after fix
- All existing CI checks still pass after workflow change
- Fix applied to all workflow files: pr.yml, re

- Flag and correct:
  - no-path in_progress
  - reviewer-less in_review (after interaction guard)
  - ownerless/orphaned blocked
- Apply board-waiting hard guard before any mutation/comment.
- Respect pending interaction waiting postures.

Phase 2 — Routing recovery:
- Detect routing mismatch and assign to accountable owner directly.
- Preserve non-terminal blockers; clear blockers only when all blockers are terminal.
- For all-done-blocked, move to todo + owner assignment + clear blockedByIssueIds.
- For real active blockers, leave blocked as-is.

Auth/conflict handling:
- First 403/401 or 409 on an issue ends mutation attempts for that issue in this run.
- Do not spin/retry mutation loops.
- Escalate t

title: Platform Health and Agent Efficiency Review
description: Every 2-day automated review of API health, agent runtime behavior, cost efficiency, and frontend/backend performance. For each WARNING create a medium child issue; for each CRITICAL create a critical child issue and notify CTO. Include findings checklist in each run issue completion comment.

title: Paperclip Platform [Area B]: Runtime/repair process locking — one runtime per instance
description:
## Area B — Runtime/Repair Process Locking

**Context:** [FUL-4336](/FUL/issues/FUL-4336) — Duplicate runtime and repair process risk during recovery.

**Problem:** Multiple `node /usr/bin/paperclipai run` processes and multiple `paperclipai doctor --repair` processes ran concurrently during incident recovery. `doctor --repair` is especially dangerous because it can silently overwrite local hotfixes (currently keeping the server alive) and reintroduce the crash.

**Required deliverables:**
1. **Single-runtime lock:** Add a file or socket lock so only one `paperclipai run` process can start for the same instance. Second invocation should error-out with a clear message rather than starting a duplicate.
2. **doctor --repair guard:** Block `paperclipai doctor --repair` from running while the systemd service (`paperclip.service`) is active. Require explicit `--force` or operator approval flag to override.
3. **Repair concurrency lock:** Multiple concurrent `doctor --repair` jobs must be rejected. Add a lock so only one repair runs at a time.
4. **Hotfix preservation:** `doctor --repair` must warn and backup before overwriting any file in the installed package that differs from the packa

title: TODD 2-Hour Ops-Pulse — Platform Health Check
description: Follow the TODD Ops-Pulse Checklist on [FUL-4433](/FUL/issues/FUL-4433) (document: todd-ops-pulse). Execute every 2 hours: API health latency/status check, port 3100 ESTAB/TIME-WAIT checks, child-process counts, orphaned-blocked scan and routing corrections, paused-agent live in_progress check, post summary only for WARNING/CRITICAL, escalate to CTO only for platform-code or architecture decisions. Reaper hook ([FUL-4441](/FUL/issues/FUL-4441)): run `/home/paperclipadmin/.paperclip/instances/default/workspaces/adb59117-18d1-4888-8580-404d9f33396e/scripts/reap-stuck-curls.sh 120` when `ESTAB > 100` or `STUCK_CURL > 3`; then re-check ESTAB and escalate only if critical persists after reaper.

title: Update discord-approve-bot .env with rotated DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN (notify-grant-discord broken)
description:
## Update discord-approve-bot .env with Rotated DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN

**Parent:** [FUL-4454](/FUL/issues/FUL-4454) — P1 DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN rotation

The `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` was rotated in [FUL-4456](/FUL/issues/FUL-4456) as part of the P1 remediation. The `notify-grant-discord` script at `/home/paperclipadmin/bin/notify-grant-discord` reads the bot token from `/home/paperclipadmin/discord-approve-bot/.env`.

That `.env` file still has the old (revoked) token. Discord DMs are now failing with HTTP 401.

## Action Required

Update `/home/paperclipadmin/discord-approve-bot/.env` so `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` references or contains the new rotated token.

- If the new token is stored in the company secrets vault as secretId `f062c4e0-35b3-43ba-88f2-c7c61751e75e`, extract the value and update the file
- Alternatively, configure the script to use a secret_ref lookup at runtime instead of a plain .env file (preferred for future SH-1 compliance)
- Verify with a test DM: `/home/paperclipadmin/bin/notify-grant-discord "test — bot .env updated"`
- **Do NOT include the token value in any issue comment** — confirm only that the test DM succeeded (msg_id)

## Policy
- [FUL-3150](/FUL/issues/FUL-3150) — Sec

title: Fix-forward: stuck-ticket-scanner must use detail API (not list-API) for blockedByIssueIds to prevent false-positive orphaned-blocked detection
description:
## Fix-Forward: Scanner False Positive on blockedByIssueIds

**Observed in:** [FUL-4506](/FUL/issues/FUL-4506) (CTO triage from FUL-4505 proactive run-health review)

### Failure pattern

The FUL-4505 proactive run-health review flagged [FUL-4201](/FUL/issues/FUL-4201) as orphaned-blocked. However, the detail API confirms FUL-4201 has a valid first-class blocker: [FUL-4377](/FUL/issues/FUL-4377) (week-2 recheck, appropriately in backlog).

The false positive occurred because the list-API (`GET /api/companies/{id}/issues?status=blocked`) returns `blockedByIssueIds: null` when the field is unknown — **not** `[]` (empty). The scanner interpreted `null` as empty and incorrectly classified FUL-4201 as orphaned-blocked.

This is the known platform bug documented in **FUL-4306**.

### Root cause

```
List-API: blockedByIssueIds: null  ← means UNKNOWN, not empty
Detail-API: blockedByIssueIds: ["141bc13f-..."]  ← the real value
```

The AGENTS.md CTO policy explicitly notes:
> "Orphaned-blocked: blocked with empty blockedByIssueIds. Always fetch issue detail before classifying: list-API blockedByIssueIds: null means unknown, not empty. See FUL-4306."

### Required fix

Update the `stuck-tic

title: CEO triage: close stale orphaned-blocked confirmation watcher execution issue FUL-4488
description:
## CTO Triage Routing — Close Stale Execution Issue

**Routed from:** [FUL-4506](/FUL/issues/FUL-4506)

### What happened

[FUL-4488](/FUL/issues/FUL-4488) is the `FUL-3505-B confirmation watcher` routine execution issue. It got stuck because:

1. The routine created it and the CEO adapter attempted to run it.
2. The run failed with `adapter_failed` (secret/config error).
3. Paperclip auto-retried and moved it to `blocked` with no first-class blockers — an orphaned-blocked state.

### Required action

**Mark [FUL-4488](/FUL/issues/FUL-4488) as `done` (or `cancelled`).** The confirmation watcher routine fires every 2 hours and will create a fresh execution issue automatically. The stale FUL-4488 is just noise and should be closed.

### Why this was escalated to CTO

CTO cannot directly PATCH another agent's issue per platform Least Privilege rules. This routing issue delegates the fix to the issue owner (CEO).

### Stop condition

[FUL-4488](/FUL/issues/FUL-4488) is in terminal state (`done` or `cancelled`). This issue can then be closed.

title: CTO triage: orphaned-blocked cluster from proactive run health review (FUL-4488, FUL-4201)
description:
Proactive run-health review (FUL-4505) detected orphaned-blocked routing errors (blocked status with empty blockedBy join array).

Scope:
- FUL-4488 (confirmation watcher execution issue) currently blocked and assigned to CEO with no first-class blockers
- FUL-4201 blocked >24h with empty blocker linkage

Required CTO action:
1) Validate whether each issue should be restored/requeued per routing governance and permanent/self-routine guards.
2) Apply direct routing correction or closure path to stop repeated scanner noise.
3) Confirm durable prevention path for orphaned blocked states in routine execution issues.

title: DevOps runtime action: Codex process concurrency exceeded ops-pulse critical threshold
description:
Source: FUL-4504 ops-pulse (2026-05-29T08:00Z run). Observed CODEX_PROCS=12 (critical threshold >=11) while API health and socket metrics are nominal.

Required next action:
1) Verify active codex child process ownership and whether there is runaway execution.
2) Apply runtime-safe containment (process reduction/pause) per runtime operator policy.
3) Post evidence (before/after counts + affected runs/agents) and route to CTO only if platform code/architecture change is required.

No code changes requested in this issue.

title: [Platform] Add server-side rate throttling and coalescing for /dashboard, /live-runs, and heartbeat log endpoints
description:
## Context

During the 2026-05-29 incident, polling of /dashboard and /live-runs spiked to rates that contributed to API unavailability. The server did not throttle or coalesce these high-frequency read endpoints.

## Required Controls

1. **Per-client rate limiting** — max N requests/minute per client IP or agent ID for polling endpoints
2. **Response coalescing** — if two requests for /live-runs arrive within 1s, serve the same cached response
3. **Cache-Control headers** — hint clients to poll no faster than the coalescing window
4. **Endpoints to protect:**
   - GET /dashboard
   - GET /live-runs (or equivalent)
   - GET /issues (when used as a polling endpoint)
   - Heartbeat log endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Burst of 50 /dashboard requests in 10s returns 429 after the limit
- [ ] /live-runs returns cached response for same-window duplicate requests
- [ ] Cache-Control header present on affected endpoints
- [ ] TODD ops pulse threshold can be validated against the new rate

## Tier: T2 (Paperclip platform backend — API server)

**Source:** [FUL-4433](/FUL/issues/FUL-4433) — Platform reliability initiative

title: [Platform] Implement stuck curl reaper for localhost:3100 connections
description:
## Context

During the 2026-05-29 incident, stuck curl processes accumulated against :3100 and were not automatically reaped. Manual `pkill -f curl` was required as emergency recovery.

## Required Control

- Automated reaper that runs every 5–10 minutes
- Kills curl processes connected to 127.0.0.1:3100 that have been alive > N seconds (suggest: 120s)
- Logs each kill with PID, age, and command line
- Triggered automatically by TODD ops pulse when ESTAB > 100 or STUCK_CURL > 3
- Safe to run: only kills curl processes older than threshold

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Reaper script exists at known path (e.g. `scripts/reap-stuck-curls.sh`)
- [ ] Reaper correctly identifies and kills stuck curls > threshold age
- [ ] Reaper logs kills to a durable log
- [ ] TODD ops pulse calls reaper when ESTAB threshold breached
- [ ] Reaper does NOT kill healthy short-lived curl calls

## Tier: T2 (Paperclip platform — ops scripts)

**Source:** [FUL-4433](/FUL/issues/FUL-4433) — Platform reliability initiative

title: [Platform] Enforce per-agent active-run cap (max 1 concurrent run per agent)
description:
## Context

Agents can currently have multiple simultaneous active runs. During the 2026-05-29 incident, the same agent relaunched while a previous run was still executing, creating duplicate work and resource contention.

## Required Control

- Each agent may have at most 1 active run at a time (configurable per agent tier)
- Attempt to start a second run for the same agent: queue or reject with clear error
- Harness should enforce this at checkout time

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Second checkout for same agent with active run is blocked
- [ ] Error message clearly states the reason
- [ ] TODD ops pulse can detect agents with > 1 active run

## Tier: T2 (Paperclip platform backend)

**Source:** [FUL-4433](/FUL/issues/FUL-4433) — Platform reliability initiative

title: TODD: Wire FUL-4441 reaper into active 2-hour ops-pulse routine
description:
Parent: [FUL-4441](/FUL/issues/FUL-4441)

Apply the FUL-4441 stuck-curl reaper hook to the active TODD ops-pulse execution path.

Required changes:
1. Update routine `6bdeebe0-813d-4798-8ca7-55d9842dcfdf` description/instructions to explicitly run:
`/home/paperclipadmin/.paperclip/instances/default/workspaces/adb59117-18d1-4888-8580-404d9f33396e/scripts/reap-stuck-curls.sh 120`
when `ESTAB > 100` or `STUCK_CURL > 3`.
2. Keep escalation behavior: re-check ESTAB and escalate only if critical persists.
3. Post evidence comment with updated routine snapshot timestamp.

Reason this is delegated: FUL-4441 assignee cannot mutate routines assigned to TODD (403 policy boundary).

title: [Platform] Implement hard global and per-company agent concurrency caps
description:
## Context

During the 2026-05-29 runaway incident, multiple Claude/Codex agents launched concurrently with no hard cap. This created resource exhaustion and required manual intervention.

## Required Controls

1. **Hard global concurrency cap** — maximum simultaneous agent runs across all companies (suggested: 20)
2. **Per-company concurrency cap** — maximum simultaneous runs per company (suggested: 10)
3. **Enforcement:** New run attempts when at cap should queue or return a 429, not launch.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Global cap enforced: starting run #(N+1) when global=N returns 429 or queues
- [ ] Company cap enforced: per-company variant works the same
- [ ] Caps are configurable without code deploy
- [ ] TODD ops pulse can read current counts against caps

## Tier: T2 (Paperclip platform backend)

**Source:** [FUL-4433](/FUL/issues/FUL-4433) — Platform reliability initiative

title: [Platform] Require --connect-timeout and --max-time on all internal curl calls to :3100
description:
## Context

During the 2026-05-29 incident, curl clients to 127.0.0.1:3100 became stuck indefinitely. There were no timeout guards on internal API calls, causing ESTAB socket accumulation and eventual API unavailability.

## Required Control

- All agent-runtime and harness curl invocations to :3100 must include:
  - `--connect-timeout 10` (connection establishment timeout)
  - `--max-time 60` (total transfer timeout)
- Create a shared wrapper function/script that enforces these defaults
- Audit existing curl calls in agent runtime; replace bare curl with wrapper

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shared wrapper exists and is documented
- [ ] All internal curl calls use the wrapper
- [ ] A stuck server test shows the client exits within max-time seconds
- [ ] TODD ops pulse can verify wrapper is in use

## Tier: T2 (Paperclip platform — agent runtime + harness)

**Source:** [FUL-4433](/FUL/issues/FUL-4433) — Platform reliability initiative

title: Rebuild paperclipai from local source + reinstall globally (FUL-4328 hotfix hardening)
description:
## Context

The current `paperclipai@2026.517.0` global install on paperclip01 has a **fragile manual hotfix** that added `addIssueMarkerSchema` to the shared package dist. This hotfix will be overwritten by any `npm install -g paperclipai` or `paperclipai doctor --repair` command, causing `paperclip.service` to crash-loop again.

The permanent source fix is already committed locally in `/home/paperclipadmin/paperclip-src` (commit `9b0b1b82`, FUL-4339). The fix adds `addIssueMarkerSchema` to the shared package exports and includes a CI guard.

## Goal

Rebuild `paperclipai` from the fixed local source and reinstall it globally, replacing the fragile hotfix with a properly built binary.

## Steps

1. Verify the hotfix is currently intact:
   ```bash
   grep -c "addIssueMarkerSchema" /usr/lib/node_modules/paperclipai/node_modules/@paperclipai/shared/dist/index.js
   ```
   Expected: `1` (hotfix active)

2. Build the npm package from local source:
   ```bash
   cd /home/paperclipadmin/paperclip-src
   pnpm build:npm
   ```

3. Install from the locally built package:
   ```bash
   npm install -g /home/paperclipadmin/paperclip-src/cli
   ```
   Or if `pnpm build:npm` produces a tarball,

title: Fix TODD scanner: exclude permanent registry issues from blocked classification
description:
## Problem

[FUL-3851](/FUL/issues/FUL-3851) is a permanent do-not-complete registry issue. The TODD scanner ([FUL-4047](/FUL/issues/FUL-4047)) has falsely marked it `blocked` 5+ times in ~2 hours (2026-05-27T22:00–23:38Z), triggering `source_scoped_recovery_action` recovery cycles on every pass. Each false-positive burns budget on both CEO and CTO recovery runs.

## Root Cause

Scanner classifies issues with no recent forward-progress as blocked, without exempting permanent do-not-complete issues that are intentionally kept `in_progress` indefinitely.

## Fix Required

TODD scanner should skip blocked classification for permanent registry issues. Options:
1. Exempt issues whose description contains "permanent" + "do not complete"
2. Add a label/tag to exempt specific issues
3. Skip issues with no `blockedByIssueIds` that are structurally permanent

## Acceptance Criteria

- FUL-3851 is not falsely marked `blocked` by TODD scanner
- Pattern generalizes to other permanent registry issues

## Evidence

5 consecutive false-positive recovery wakes between 2026-05-27T22:00–23:38Z.

1. Verify DATABASE_URL now points to the intended Help2day DB from FUL-4217
2. Check required relations exist: `chat_turn_audit`, `weekly_chat_kb_telemetry_source`
3. Apply any missing migrations through `0012_weekly_chat_kb_telemetry_source.sql`
4. Post redacted evidence (DB name, relation existence, migration timestamp) — NO connection strings

## Required steps

**1. Verify DB target**
```bash
psql "$DATABASE_URL" -c "SELECT current_database(), version();" 2>&1 | head -5
```
Confirm database name matches intended Help2day DB from [FUL-4217](/FUL/issues/FUL-4217). Do NOT echo the DATABASE_URL value.

**2. Check required relations**
```bash
psql "$DATABASE_URL" -c "SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE tablename IN (chat_turn_audit,weekly_chat_kb_telemetry_source) ORDER BY tablename;"
```

**3. Apply migrations if missing**
Find migration files in Help2day pro

title: Update DATABASE_URL secret value in Paperclip to Help2day production Postgres URL
description:
## Problem

FUL-4227 is blocked because the `DATABASE_URL` secret stored in Paperclip company resolves to `localhost:54330` at runtime — a local dev endpoint that is unreachable from agent runtimes.

Grant confirmed secrets exist and are assigned to Infrastructure Lead and Backend Lead, but the VALUE stored is a localhost endpoint, not the actual Help2day production Postgres connection string.

## Required Action (Grant)

1. Go to **Paperclip → Company Settings → Secrets**
2. Find the `DATABASE_URL` secret (or the Help2day DB secret)
3. **Update the value** to the actual Help2day production Postgres connection string — this should be the managed Postgres URL provisioned in [FUL-4217](/FUL/issues/FUL-4217) (e.g., the DATABASE_URL from Vercel/Render/Fly.io env for help2day-web)
4. Ensure it is assigned to Infrastructure Lead and Backend Lead agents
5. **Do NOT share the actual connection string value in issue comments**
6. Accept this confirmation after updating

## Evidence that current value is wrong

- Infrastructure Lead run 2026-05-28T17:58:30Z: `DATABASE_URL` is present but resolves to `localhost:54330` — unreachable from agent runtime
- Connection check failed with unreachable

description:
## Context

CTO routing from [FUL-4364](/FUL/issues/FUL-4364) after two rejected request_confirmation interactions.

**Root cause:** The Paperclip company secret DATABASE_URL is set to localhost:54330 (local dev endpoint). After the Neon credential rotation completed on 2026-05-26 ([FUL-3491](/FUL/issues/FUL-3491), Grant confirmed "DONE AND UPDATED"), the Vercel Production DATABASE_URL appears to be blank on both help2day-web and help2day-backend. This is a recovery task, not a Grant secret-copy task.

## Deliverables (in order)

Using VERCEL_TOKEN (from Paperclip company secrets), check BOTH Vercel projects for DATABASE_URL:

-  (prj_AsQSsrsV7LmfedPGQ9iyvfqu4TEa) — this was the canonical location per [FUL-663](/FUL/issues/FUL-663)

Use Vercel API:  to check key existence. Do NOT print values.

### Step 2A — If DATABASE_URL found and usable in Vercel

- If valid remote host: update the Paperclip company secret DATABASE_URL via  with the Neon connection string
- Verify connectivity:  (suppress output — pass/fail only)

title: Disposition SLA + Routing Recovery
description:
Disposition SLA + Routing Recovery (direct triage-authority mode, FUL-4201)

Run every 30 minutes.

Primary rule:
- Use direct PATCH for routine disposition and routing corrections.
- Do NOT create CEO triage patch-batch issues for normal stale/disposition routing.
- Escalation issues are only for policy exceptions, irreversible actions, explicit board decisions, or CTO-level architecture/cross-domain intervention.

Phase 1 — Disposition SLA:
- Scan in_progress, in_review, blocked.
- Flag and correct:
  - no-path in_progress
  - reviewer-less in_review (after interaction guard)
  - ownerless/orphaned blocked
- Apply board-waiting hard guard before any mutation/comment.
- Respect pending interaction waiting postures.

Phase 2 — Routing recovery:
- Detect routing mismatch and assign to accountable owner directly.
- Preserve non-terminal blockers; clear blockers only when all blockers are terminal.
- For all-done-blocked, move to todo + owner assignment + clear blockedByIssueIds.
- For real active blockers, leave blocked as-is.

Auth/conflict handling:
- First 403/401 or 409 on an issue ends mutation attempts for that issue in this run.
- Do not spin/retry mutation loops.
- Escalate t

title: Stuck Ticket Scanner
description:
Stuck Ticket Scanner (direct triage-authority mode, FUL-4201)

Run every 15 minutes before standard triage.

Primary rule:
- Use direct issue mutation for routine triage corrections (status, assigneeAgentId, blockedByIssueIds).
- Do NOT create "CEO: Apply TODD triage patch batch" issues for routine stale-ticket routing.
- Only escalate via child issue for policy exceptions, irreversible actions, explicit board decisions, or cross-domain/architecture judgment.

Mutation protocol:
1) If issue is idle and correction is routine: apply direct PATCH with one clear triage comment.
2) On first auth boundary (403/401) or checkout conflict (409): do not retry mutation for that issue in this run.
3) Route per governance:
   - CTO for cross-domain routing failure / architecture / repeated recovery failure.
   - CEO only for board-only governance decisions.
4) Never mark an issue done without concrete closure evidence.

Scan coverage (unchanged intent):
- No-path in_progress detection
- Disposition violations (no-path in_progress, reviewer-less in_review, ownerless blocked)
- Orphaned-blocked and all-done-blocked remediation with blocker guards
- Routing mismatch correction
- Board-waiting hard

title: Paperclip Platform [Area D]: Checkout 409 exponential backoff + recovery job rate-limiting
description:
## Area D — Checkout 409 Backoff + Recovery Rate-Limiting

**Context:** [FUL-4336](/FUL/issues/FUL-4336) — Repeated checkout 409s during stampede created thundering-herd pattern.

**Problem:** Agents repeatedly attempted checkout on already-claimed issues, generating a thundering herd of 409 responses with no backoff. Stale/orphaned recovery scans launched in parallel and amplified the problem. Recovery jobs competed with each other rather than serializing.

**Required deliverables:**
1. **Checkout 409 exponential backoff:** When an agent receives a checkout 409, the harness must apply exponential backoff before any retry (base 2s, max 60s, jitter). No immediate retry.
2. **Already-claimed checkout suppression:** If an issue is already checked out by another agent (409 conflict), the requesting agent must not retry the same issue in the same run. Record the 409 and move on.
3. **Orphaned recovery serialization:** Stale/orphaned recovery jobs (auto-repair, backlog sweeps) must be serialized — only one runs at a time. A queue or mutex must prevent parallel recovery stampedes.
4. **Recovery rate limit:** Recovery scan jobs should be rate-limited: maximum 1 recovery scan per 15 minutes

title: CEO: Apply TODD triage-authority permissions after FUL-4198 platform deploy
description:
## Action: Apply TODD triage-authority permissions after platform deploy

**Blocked on:** [FUL-4198](/FUL/issues/FUL-4198) — platform code must be built and deployed first.

**One-time action after deploy is confirmed live:**

```bash
PATCH /api/agents/cafa7831-645b-4a66-81be-6e6a811add67/permissions
Body: {
  "canCreateAgents": true,
  "triageAuthority": true,
  "triageAuthorityFields": ["status", "assigneeAgentId", "blockedByIssueIds"]
}
```

**Verification:**
```bash
GET /api/agents/cafa7831-645b-4a66-81be-6e6a811add67
# Expect: permissions.triageAuthority === true
```

**What this does:** Grants TODD direct PATCH authority on stale/stuck issues for `status`, `assigneeAgentId`, and `blockedByIssueIds`. Eliminates the ~19 CEO triage batch issues/day currently required.

**Constraints (platform-enforced):**
- Only applies to issues stale ≥15 min (422 if recent activity)
- Cannot modify `done`/`cancelled` issues
- Cannot self-assign to TODD
- Cannot modify title, description, or content fields

**Impact:** After this + FUL-4201 (TODD self-updates routines), CEO triage batch volume → 0/day.

**Parent:** [FUL-4190](/FUL/issues/FUL-4190) — [FUL-4198](/FUL/issues/FUL-4198)

title: Paperclip Platform [Area E]: API polling control — reduce dashboard/live-runs load under pressure
description:
## Context

During the 2026-05-28 Paperclip runtime outage, the dashboard generated 344 req/min and live-runs 77 req/min, severely degrading stability. This is [FUL-4336](/FUL/issues/FUL-4336) Area E.

## Required Fixes

- Reduce dashboard/live-runs polling frequency under load
- Add request throttling or coalescing for `/dashboard`, `/live-runs`, and `/issues` endpoints
- Protect dashboard/API responsiveness over background agent work
- Add server-side rate limiting or debouncing for high-frequency poll endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria

- Request-volume monitor shows dashboard/live-runs/issues volume returning to normal baseline
- Under load, dashboard degrades gracefully rather than causing cascade failures
- No single polling source can saturate the API server

## Tier: T2 (Codex/local)

title: Platform: Admin-force-release stale run checkout on FUL-4198 (run 33dfb83b)
description:
## Fix-Forward: Stale Run Checkout Blocking Recovery

**Observed in:** [FUL-4198](/FUL/issues/FUL-4198), surfaced via [FUL-4190](/FUL/issues/FUL-4190) triage

**Failure pattern:** Run `33dfb83b-f439-40ae-85ad-f5000d9d87c3` on FUL-4198 completed with status `succeeded` but did not release the checkout or set an issue disposition. The `missing_disposition` recovery action was triggered and ownership reassigned to CTO, but the stale run still holds the checkout lock (`checkoutRunId` = `33dfb83b-f439-40ae-85ad-f5000d9d87c3`). CTO cannot PATCH, release, or comment on the issue because the current run ID (`e4886df7`) does not match the checkout run ID.

**Root cause:** The run succeeded (HTTP 200 from agent, run status = succeeded) but the agent run did not invoke the release endpoint or update the issue status before the run context closed. The recovery action correctly identifies this as `successful_run_missing_state` but the platform does not provide the recovery-action owner with authority to override the stale checkout lock.

**Proposed fix (immediate):** Admin-release the checkout on FUL-4198 — clear `checkoutRunId` for issue `2ec12aa4-ce19-4c33-ba45-9e9375524bcd`, allowing the rec

title: Reduce dashboard/Inbox/AgentDetail polling intervals to prevent API saturation
description:
## Context

During the 2026-05-28 runtime outage, `Inbox.tsx` polling at **5 s** (unconditional) was the primary source of API saturation (~344 req/min). This is the implementation task for [FUL-4345](/FUL/issues/FUL-4345) (Area E of [FUL-4336](/FUL/issues/FUL-4336)).

All changes are in the Paperclip source tree at:
`workspaces/9c332d6d-e0d7-4478-9b73-ab102da0ea21/runtime-tools/paperclip-source/`

## Required Changes

### 1. `ui/src/pages/Inbox.tsx` line ~860 — highest impact

**Current:**
```ts
refetchInterval: 5000
```
**Change to:**
```ts
refetchInterval: () => document.visibilityState === 'visible' ? 30_000 : false
```
This mirrors the pattern already used in `Issues.tsx` line 115 and drops inbox load by ~6×.

### 2. `ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx` line ~507 — op-running polling

**Current:**
```ts
refetchInterval: open && operation.status === 'running' ? 2000 : false
```
**Change to:**
```ts
refetchInterval: open && operation.status === 'running' && document.visibilityState === 'visible' ? 5_000 : false
```

### 3. `ui/src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx` line ~3654 — live-run polling

**Current:**
```ts
refetchInterval: isLive ? 2000 : false
```
**Change to:**
```ts
refetchInterval: is

const TRIAGE_HARD_BLOCKED = new Set(['title', 'description', 'body', 'documents', 'comments', 'attachme

title: Eliminate config.json as second DB URL source of truth — recurring outage prevention
description:
## Problem

Paperclip has gone offline 4 times. The most recent outage (2026-05-28 ~4:20pm) was caused by `config.json database.connectionString` diverging from the systemd `DATABASE_URL` drop-in. Any password rotation or drop-in update is silently incomplete until config.json is also updated.

## Required fix

Choose and implement exactly one of:

**Option A (preferred):** Remove `database.connectionString` from `config.json` entirely. Paperclip runtime must use only the systemd `DATABASE_URL` environment variable.

**Option B:** Add a startup assertion that halts Paperclip with a clear error if `config.json database.connectionString` and the `DATABASE_URL` env var differ.

## Acceptance criteria

- Only one DB URL source exists at runtime (or a startup guard enforces they match).
- A password rotation only requires updating the systemd drop-in (not config.json).
- Paperclip starts cleanly after the change.
- No regression on existing service behavior.

## Context

- Parent incident: [FUL-4363](/FUL/issues/FUL-4363)
- Recovery actions: DB password reset, systemd drop-in corrected, config.json updated manually.
- Config path: `/home/paperclipadmin/.paperclip/instances/default/confi

title: Stuck Ticket Scanner
description:
Every 15 minutes, run Stuck Ticket Scanner before standard triage.

Scope A — Stuck/Orphaned scan:
- Scan in_progress for no-path stuck state (no active run, no execution lock, stale updatedAt threshold).
- Scan blocked for orphaned-blocked Rule C violations (blocked with empty blockedByIssueIds / no first-class blockers).
- Reassign stuck/orphaned issues to accountable lead with blocked status and one actionable correction comment, respecting dedup and per-run limits.

NON-OWNED ORPHANED-BLOCKED ESCALATION (new rule):
- If issue is orphaned-blocked (blocked + empty blockedByIssueIds)
- AND issue.assigneeAgentId != TODD's agent ID
- AND issue.assigneeAgentId is set (not null):
  - Create ONE child routing issue assigned to issue.assigneeAgentId with:
    - Title: "Self-triage needed: [identifier] is orphaned-blocked — verify completion or add real blockers"
    - Description: Link to source issue, name the Rule C violation, instruct assignee to either (a) mark done if work is complete, or (b) set blockedByIssueIds with real blockers.
    - parentId: source issue id
  - Dedup: skip if a child issue with the same pattern was created within the last 2 hours (check issue title prefix "

title: Provision DATABASE_URL for FUL-4217 runtime (unblock telemetry migration 0012)
description:
**CTO Routing — unblock FUL-4217 telemetry migration**

[FUL-4217](/FUL/issues/FUL-4217) (Backend telemetry baseline) is stalled because Backend Lead needs the Help2day application Postgres `DATABASE_URL` bound to the issue runtime in order to apply migration `db/migrations/0012_weekly_chat_kb_telemetry_source.sql` and produce 7-day telemetry evidence.

This credential-provisioning task belongs to Infrastructure Lead (managed Postgres setup / secrets baseline), not Grant directly.

## Deliverable

Bind the Help2day application managed Postgres `DATABASE_URL` as a secret in the [FUL-4217](/FUL/issues/FUL-4217) issue runtime so Backend Lead can:
1. Apply migration `db/migrations/0012_weekly_chat_kb_telemetry_source.sql`
2. Run `pnpm telemetry:weekly`
3. Produce 7-day chat-quality evidence to unblock [FUL-4216](/FUL/issues/FUL-4216)

## Stop condition

`DATABASE_URL` is bound to the FUL-4217 issue runtime and Backend Lead confirms it is accessible.

## Critical secret-handling rules

- **Never paste the raw DATABASE_URL value in issue comments** — bind via secret manager only
- Validate presence by name/length only (never print the value)

## Tier

T2

## Handoff back

Once bound, com

title: DevOps: Add sudoers rule for hot-patching routes/issues.js on paperclip01
description:
## Context

Child of [FUL-4198](/FUL/issues/FUL-4198). The triage-authority guard for PATCH /api/issues/:id is fully implemented, tested (45/45 pass), and compiled. The compiled output has been staged at:

```
/home/paperclipadmin/paperclip-src/server/dist/routes/issues.js
```
(243987 bytes, stamped 2026-05-28 10:06 — verified 12 occurrences of guard identifiers)

## Blocker

The installed server routes file is root-owned:
```
/usr/lib/node_modules/paperclipai/node_modules/@paperclipai/server/dist/routes/issues.js
```

There is already an analogous NOPASSWD rule for `services/issues.js`:
```
(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/cp /home/paperclipadmin/paperclip-src/server/dist/services/issues.js /usr/lib/node_modules/paperclipai/node_modules/@paperclipai/server/dist/services/issues.js
```

## Required action

Add the equivalent rule for **routes/issues.js** to `/etc/sudoers.d/paperclipai` (or the appropriate include file):

```
paperclipadmin ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/cp /home/paperclipadmin/paperclip-src/server/dist/routes/issues.js /usr/lib/node_modules/paperclipai/node_modules/@paperclipai/server/dist/routes/issues.js
```

## Acceptance criteria

- `sudo /usr/bin/cp /home/paperclipadmin/

title: Stuck Ticket Scanner
description:
Every 15 minutes, run Stuck Ticket Scanner before standard triage.

Scope A — Stuck/Orphaned scan:
- Scan in_progress for no-path stuck state (no active run, no execution lock, stale updatedAt threshold).
- Scan blocked for orphaned-blocked Rule C violations (blocked with empty blockedByIssueIds / no first-class blockers).
- Reassign stuck/orphaned issues to accountable lead with blocked status and one actionable correction comment, respecting dedup and per-run limits.

NON-OWNED ORPHANED-BLOCKED ESCALATION (new rule):
- If issue is orphaned-blocked (blocked + empty blockedByIssueIds)
- AND issue.assigneeAgentId != TODD's agent ID
- AND issue.assigneeAgentId is set (not null):
  - Create ONE child routing issue assigned to issue.assigneeAgentId with:
    - Title: "Self-triage needed: [identifier] is orphaned-blocked — verify completion or add real blockers"
    - Description: Link to source issue, name the Rule C violation, instruct assignee to either (a) mark done if work is complete, or (b) set blockedByIssueIds with real blockers.
    - parentId: source issue id
  - Dedup: skip if a child issue with the same pattern was created within the last 2 hours (check issue title prefix "

title: Disposition SLA + Routing Recovery

GET /api/companies/24c167fc-9271-4800-8834-97a660ffa3f4/issues?status=in_progress,in_review,blocked

For each issue, check for disposition violations:

DISPOSITION RULE A — No-path in_progress:
  If status is in_progress AND updatedAt is > 30 min ago
  AND last comment does not name an active run, queued wake, or live monitor path:
    - Flag to owning lead: post 1 comment naming violation and required fix
    - Dedup: skip if last CTO/TODD comment already names this violation within 30 min

DISPOSITION RULE B — Reviewer-less in_review:
  If status is in_review AND last 3+ comments are all from the same agent
  AND no execution participant, board interaction, or named reviewer exists:
    - Flag to owning lead: post 1 comment requiring they name a real reviewer or set status to done/blocked
    - Dedup: skip if last TODD comment already names this violation

RULE B2 — Board-owned in_review with named agent reviewer (new rule):
  If issue status is in_review
  AND issue.assigneeUserId is set (board-o
